summary: Follow-up readiness review after plan corrections: the previously identified executable blocker and stale closeout ambiguities are resolved. The plan now imports TokenRef from elspeth.contracts.audit, correctly documents the all-diverted derived-status fallback as FAILED rather than EMPTY, requires real Filigree follow-up issue IDs instead of relying on unverified observation IDs, and instructs the hashlib import in ruff/isort-compatible order.

resolved_findings.issue: Audit test snippet imported TokenRef from elspeth.contracts even though TokenRef is defined in elspeth.contracts.audit and not re-exported by contracts/__init__.py.
resolved_findings.resolution: Plan now imports TokenRef from elspeth.contracts.audit.

resolved_findings.issue: Predicate-asymmetry prose described an all-sink-diverted processed run as EMPTY or an EMPTY boundary raise, contradicting current and planned derive_terminal_run_status behavior.
resolved_findings.resolution: Plan now states that processed rows with no success indicator classify as FAILED, while keeping the broader all-diverted taxonomy question out of scope.

resolved_findings.issue: Closeout text referenced unverified ambient observation IDs, making tracker hygiene depend on stale or undiscoverable identifiers.
resolved_findings.resolution: Plan now requires creating or promoting real Filigree follow-up issues before closeout and recording explicit issue IDs.

resolved_findings.issue: The outcomes.py import instruction placed import hashlib after a from-import, which could steer the implementer into ruff I001/import-order churn.
resolved_findings.resolution: Plan now tells the implementer to add import hashlib in standard-library ruff/isort order before the existing from collections.abc import Iterable import.

implementation_notes: This approval covers readiness of the plan text, not execution success. Implementation still must follow the plan's RED/GREEN sequence, final full-worktree gates, and Filigree closeout requirements.
The plan remains intentionally explicit that intermediate task commits may be compile-broken and must not be pushed as integration-ready history.
